summary:
Late-night salivary cortisol has higher false positivity rates in obese patients with type 2 diabetes due to overlapping metabolic conditions that mimic hypercortisolemia. Therefore, standard screening methods remain more reliable for detecting true cortisol excess in these individuals. 

The summary above meets all requirements — concise, focused only on clinically significant aspects of the comparison between testing modalities in the specified patient group.
